text.title: data-driven-requirements Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for data-driven-requirements as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adoption-stage mismatch: a BA practice that pushes data-driven requirements before the org has a reliable warehouse.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tag the practice's maturity (see "Adoption gates") and don't skip stages. Requirements with placeholder baselines that nobody trusts fail faster than no requirements at all.

failure-mode.description: Definition drift: "active user" means three different things in three dashboards.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a metric catalog, two BAs pull contradicting baselines for the same KPI on the same day. Keep a single metrics.md before reaching for a metric layer.

failure-mode.description: Survivorship bias: heavy users skew the data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The cohort that already churned is invisible — the requirement looks well-justified and is solving the wrong problem. Slice baselines by ≥1 dimension (channel, plan, geo).

failure-mode.description: Single-source bias: pulling from one tool (just GA, just Mixpanel) inherits that tool's tracking gaps.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-check with the system of record (DB, Stripe, support tickets) before locking a baseline.

failure-mode.description: Confidence theater: stamping a number doesn't make it correct.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always include n, as_of, and source_url next to every baseline. A number without those three is gossip.

failure-mode.description: Causal claims from correlational data: "users of feature X retain 2x" almost never means "shipping X for everyone doubles retention."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Self-selection eats the effect. Add a causal_evidence_type field; observational claims must be hedged.

failure-mode.description: Privacy/consent: requirements built on data the org cannot lawfully use under GDPR/CCPA.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fail at legal review even when statistically sound. Restrict warehouse roles, and run a redaction-checker before commit.

failure-mode.description: Dashboard fixation: BAs start prioritizing what's easy to query (page views) over what matters (retained revenue).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tie every metric to one sentence about the business outcome it maps to. If the BA can't write that sentence, the KPI is vanity.

failure-mode.description: LLMs invent plausible KPI values without citation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force every cell to cite a source URL or query, and have a verifier agent re-run the query.

failure-mode.description: Agents collapse different metric definitions ("conversion", "completion", "purchase") into a single number.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Resolve names against metrics.md first. This blocks the most common silent failure.

failure-mode.description: Date drift: an agent fetched a baseline last week and will reuse it forever.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add the freshness gate as a finalization step. Enforce as_of in the schema.

failure-mode.description: Vanity-metric capture: agents pick the easiest-to-query metric over the most-business-relevant one.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Constrain the schema to require a downstream business KPI (revenue, retention) and a "why this matters" sentence per requirement.

failure-mode.description: Sycophancy: an agent finds data supporting whatever the question implies.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a counter-evidence pass before finalization: "find the strongest signal that this requirement should NOT be built."

failure-mode.description: Privacy leak: agents may pull PII columns into the evidence section.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Restrict warehouse roles, and run a redaction-checker before commit.

failure-mode.description: Adoption-gate denial: agents will happily produce data-driven requirements at G1 with placeholder baselines.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hard-code the gate in the prompt: refuse to ship a requirement labeled "data-driven" if metrics.md has fewer than 3 KPIs.
